title: arnica alpina subsp. angustifolia
intro: Arnica alpina subsp. angustifolia, also known as alpine arnica, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to Europe and has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antirheumatic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and improving skin conditions. Herbal preparations of alpine arnica, including tinctures, creams, and ointments, can be applied topically to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, sprains, and bruises, and to soothe skin irritations and injuries.
